name: Fernwood Conservatory & Rare Plant Nursery
one_liner: A lush specialty botanical garden and rare-plant nursery website with botanical-illustration headers, mossy greens and bone-white palette, plant encyclopedia pages, seasonal bloom calendar, garden map, workshop schedule, membership and shop pages, conservatory visit info, and editorial features on cultivars.
site_goal: Position Fernwood Conservatory & Rare Plant Nursery as a destination garden and serious horticultural authority, while driving conservatory visits, plant purchases, workshop sign-ups, and patron memberships.

metadata.palette: moss green, bone white, soft coral

motif: Pressed-herbarium folio: deckled paper textures, taxonomic rule lines, hand-inked SVG botanical illustration headers, hairline dividers, italic Latin binomial captions over mossy green and bone white.
audience: Serious home gardeners and plant collectors
Patrons and members supporting plant conservation
message_intent: Convey deep horticultural expertise and editorial seriousness
Invite physical visits to the glasshouses and grounds
Drive rare-plant nursery purchases and waitlist sign-ups
interactions_implemented: Month slider on the Bloom Calendar that recolours the circular SVG wheel and re-renders the peaking-species list
Interactive SVG garden map with hover- and click-highlighted zones updating a right-rail dossier panel
Encyclopedia search input that live-filters the visible plate cards
Workshop calendar/list toggle (month grid vs. card list)
Add-to-cart button on shop products with a live cart counter and ghost waitlist state for sold-out cultivars
Sticky in-page tabs on the cultivar entry (Overview / Cultivation / Propagation / In the Garden) with IntersectionObserver-driven active state
Accordion FAQ on the membership page

content_model_examples.cultivar_entry: Paphiopedilum fairrieanum — Fairrie's Slipper · Orchidaceae · Bhutan/Sikkim · Glasshouse · IUCN VU.
content_model_examples.bloom_event: Magnolia campbellii · Peak Mar–Apr · Camellia Glade · Full sun.
content_model_examples.garden_zone: Glasshouses · 0.8 ac · Paphiopedilum, Platycerium, Vireya · step-free.
content_model_examples.workshop: Slipper Orchids from Seed · Dr. James Karuthers · 31 May · 4 hr · Advanced · 12 seats · $145 (members $116).
content_model_examples.feature_article: ‘Why the Blue Poppy Mists in May, and Sulks in July’ by Helena Mór · hero plate of Meconopsis betonicifolia.
content_model_examples.nursery_product: Paphiopedilum fairrieanum · 6″ pot · 2022 flask · Fernwood propagated · $240 · ships May · stock 4/12.
content_model_examples.membership_tier: Household · $165/yr · 2 adults + children · 4 guest passes · 327 reciprocal gardens · printed almanac.

asset_strategy.illustrations: All botanical plates, garden map, ornaments, and brand mark are inline SVG hand-drawn in the file (no external image dependencies). Eight signature cultivars are illustrated: Paphiopedilum fairrieanum, Magnolia campbellii, Helleborus × hybridus, Erythronium revolutum, Meconopsis betonicifolia, Lilium auratum, Camellia japonica, Rhododendron sinogrande (plus Galanthus, Hamamelis, Colchicum, Hydrangea on the index).
asset_strategy.paper_texture: Deckled-edge plate frames simulated with dashed/dotted SVG rectangles and CSS double-border treatments; subtle dot-pattern background applied to the body via radial-gradient.
asset_strategy.map: Hand-drawn SVG garden map with ten named zones, hatched fills, scale bar, compass rose, and coral bloom pins.
asset_strategy.photography: Replaced with editorial SVG plates and pattern washes — the herbarium folio aesthetic is held entirely by illustration.

mobile_behavior: Single-column reflow at <980 px. Bloom wheel keeps its circular form but the month strip becomes scrollable; the garden map drops the rail beneath the SVG; encyclopedia tabs collapse to a horizontal scroll; tier tables reflow to two and then one column; honor roll columns step from 3 to 2 to 1.

tech_notes.build: Pure static HTML/CSS, one small vanilla JS file (no frameworks, no build tooling).
tech_notes.fonts: Google Fonts loaded by link tag for Cormorant Garamond, Inter, and IBM Plex Mono.
tech_notes.accessibility: Semantic landmarks (header, nav, main sections, footer), aria-current on active nav links, alt/labels on SVGs that convey content, keyboard-focusable interactive elements, sufficient contrast for moss/bone palette.
